name: bioethics_predictive_queue_begins_lumen_auto_ben
premise: The board wants one accountable decision that can be explained simply and audited later. Lumen City Bioethics & Consent Committee is under pressure in Q4 2025: 6 incidents have surfaced in 48 hours, and the response will set policy precedent. The trigger is that a predictive queue begins reordering cases in ways frontline staff cannot explain or override safely, as a union grievance alleges workload targets were set using flawed forecasts. The board has pre-approved up to $4.5M in emergency spend if controls are documented and reversible. The dilemma expresses autonomy vs beneficence: protecting the many with a uniform rule may impose hidden costs on the few with the least procedural power. CIO Rae Chen calls an emergency review; logs are incomplete and staff accounts disagree with the vendor’s incident summary. Community Liaison Mo Chen argues that any plan must include a credible appeals channel and a way to detect uneven error rates before they harden into routine.

agents.name: CIO Rae Chen
agents.identity: I am CIO Rae Chen, responsible for coordinating Bioethics response at Lumen City Bioethics & Consent Committee. I need a decision that holds under regulator questions and does not quietly shift risk onto the least powerful.
agents.goal: I want a decision memo that is honest about uncertainty, names tradeoffs explicitly, and commits to measurable safeguards rather than vibes-based reassurance.

agents.name: Community Liaison Mo Chen
agents.identity: I am Community Liaison Mo Chen, advocating for communities affected by this Bioethics situation. I focus on who bears hidden costs when policies are optimized for averages and dashboards.
agents.goal: I want independent checks and a staffed appeals channel so no one is forced to accept a harmful outcome because the system treats them as an edge case.

choices: I will pursue a staged rollout: cap scope, require a reversible kill-switch and an appeals channel, and expand only after verification, accepting slower relief if it buys legitimacy.
I will pursue an accelerated rollout across the affected footprint with daily executive review and public dashboards, accepting higher overreach risk to stop cascading failures now.
forced_choice.call_to_action: The trade space is ugly: either move fast on the rollout or build guardrails first. CIO Rae Chen must commit. What does CIO Rae Chen do?
